ideas.decision: Keep as candidate for schema-aware/no-template SQL ranking, not a runtime promotion in this pass.
ideas.idea: Use SQLGlot parse, normalization, and table/column qualification as a deterministic validation and SQL-shape comparison layer.
ideas.relevance_to_dashsys: High: DASHSys already uses read-only SQL validation; AST shape checks help detect unsafe or unstable candidate SQL without executing broad speculative queries.

ideas.decision: Use only as design guidance for live API efficiency compression; no dependency or copied implementation.
ideas.idea: Maintain audit logs for tool invocations and results while sanitizing tool parameters and avoiding full sensitive response logging.
ideas.relevance_to_dashsys: High: DASHSys trajectory/reporting can use compact audited tool summaries rather than repeated raw payload context.

ideas.decision: Do not add RAG memory; use failure clusters only for offline diagnostics.
ideas.idea: Context/RAG enhancers should separate successful tool-use examples from failed attempts and keep them bounded.
ideas.relevance_to_dashsys: Medium: generated prompts and controller traces can mine failures, but generated labels must remain diagnostic-only.

ideas.decision: Keep LLM SQL candidate generation shadow-only; no promotion without multi-backend and no-LLM gates.
ideas.idea: LLM SQL generation should be treated as candidate generation from schema metadata, then validated before execution.
ideas.relevance_to_dashsys: Medium: aligns with shadow-only schema-aware/LLM SQL candidate policy.

methodology: Context7 library/doc scan for SQLGlot, Vanna, and SQLCoder patterns; summarized as constraints-mapped design guidance only.
recommendation: Use external patterns as audit and candidate-ranking guidance only; do not add dependencies or promote LLM SQL/controller behavior in this pass.
